mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate on equity cash flows and signals policy support, so a month-over-month decline in policy rates predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive more often than negative during FEDFUNDS decline regimes, or if the signal's rolling 2-year rank correlation stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above a restrictive threshold, higher real financing costs tighten financial conditions and compress risk appetite, so equity returns over the next 5 trading days turn negative after the threshold is crossed.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.0% and CPIAUCSL inflation is rising on a 3-month annualized basis
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative 5-day forward returns at least 60% of the time, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional 5-day mean.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE with falling PAYEMS indicates worsening labor market momentum, which reduces expected household income and raises recession odds, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor deterioration trigger fails to outperform the unconditional signal or if positive 20-day returns occur in more than 45% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strength in retail sales and housing starts implies resilient domestic demand and better near-term revenue growth for cyclicals, so simultaneous improvements in RSAFS and HOUST predict positive SPX returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined-demand signal does not beat either proxy alone in out-of-sample IC, or if 60-day forward returns are not positive on average when both series rise together.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with stable PAYEMS signals improving productivity and industrial capacity utilization, which supports earnings margins and produces positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises while PAYEMS is flat to slightly positive over the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if periods with rising INDPRO and non-negative PAYEMS do not generate positive 20-day returns more often than the benchmark or if the return distribution is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDPC1 signals slower aggregate output growth and weaker future earnings expectations, so quarterly GDP deceleration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not lower after GDP decelerations than after GDP accelerations, or if the rolling 3-year IC remains statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE marks labor market deterioration that undermines consumption and risk appetite, so the combined signal predicts negative SPX returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls and UNRATE rises in the same monthly release window
failure_condition: Reject if the paired deterioration signal does not generate negative 5-day returns at least 60% of the time, or if its conditional mean return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation is elevated and the policy rate is still low, real rates stay suppressed and the market reads the setup as delayed tightening, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation is above 3% while FEDFUNDS remains below 2%
failure_condition: Reject if elevated-inflation/low-rate regimes do not produce above-average 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign flips negative after controlling for the same inflation regime.
